title: ModelEngine-Group/nexent
description: Nexent is a zero-code platform for auto-generating production-grade AI agents using Harness Engineering principles — unified tools, skills, memory, and orchestration with built-in constraints, feedback loops, and control planes.

description: An awesome list of Agent Harness engineering resources, including GitHub projects, tools, benchmarks, and practical guides.

title: china-qijizhifeng/agentic-harness-engineering
description: Official AHE code — Agentic Harness Engineering: observability-driven automatic evolution of coding-agent harnesses (concurrent w/ meta-harness). NexAU-AHE reaches 84.7% ± 2.1 pass@1 on Terminal-Bench 2 (GPT-5.5). Lifts GPT-5.4 69.7→77.0% over 10 iters, beats Codex/ACE/Training-Free GRPO; frozen harness transfers to SWE-bench-Verified.

title: affaan-m/ECC
description: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.

title: bytedance/deer-flow
description: An open-source long-horizon SuperAgent harness that researches, codes, and creates. With the help of sandboxes, memories, tools, skill, subagents and message gateway, it handles different levels of tasks that could take minutes to hours.

Does Code Cleanliness Affect Coding Agents? A Controlled Minimal-Pair Study
description: As autonomous coding agents see rapid adoption, their evaluation has primarily focused on task completion rates holding the target codebase fixed. This leaves a critical question unanswered: does the structural and stylistic quality, or ``cleanliness'' of the underlying code affect an agent's ability to navigate and modify it? To isolate the effect of code cleanliness from agent capability, we int

title: Formal Skill: Programmable Runtime Skills for Efficient and Accurate LLM Agents
description: Large Language Model (LLM) agents increasingly act inside real workspaces, where tools and skills determine whether model reasoning becomes reliable action. Existing skills remain largely informal: Markdown skills and instruction packs encode procedures as long natural-language documents, while function calling, Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, and framework tools structure individual actions
authors: Xi Zhang, Meijun Gao, Yuntian Zhao
